title: $9 Million Cigna Foundation Grant for Youth Mental Health Programs (Apply by March 12, 2026)
summary: Nonprofit organizations working to support children and teenagers can now apply for the Improving Youth Mental Health Grant Program , funded by the Cigna Group Foundation . The foundation has committed $9 million over three years to support programs that improve youth mental health, emotional well-being, and access to mental health care for young people aged 5 18 . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); This funding opportunity focuses on addressing the growing mental health challenges affecting children and adolescents, particularly post-pandemic stress, anxiety, and emotional distress . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Organizations working with parents, caregivers, educators, therapists, and youth-serving professionals are also encouraged to apply. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Application Deadline: March 12, 2026 (5:00 PM ET) About the Improving Youth Mental Health Grant Program The Improving Youth Mental Health Grant Program supports nonprofit organizations delivering evidence-informed programs that help children and teens build resilience, develop social-emotional skills, and access mental health services. The initiative responds to the growing need for early intervention and mental health support among young people. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Even before the COVID-19 pandemic, one in five children aged 3 17 experienced a mental, emotional, developmental, or behavioral disorder . Since the pandemic, the need for effective mental health support systems for youth has increased significantly. The Cigna Foundation aims to partner with organizations that can deliver scalable solutions, strengthen community support systems, and expand access to care for underserved youth populations . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Grant Focus Areas Nonprofits applying for funding must align their programs with at least one of the following priority goals : 1. Strengthening Social-Emotional Skills Programs that help youth develop skills related to: Emotional regulation Stress management Resilience Mental well-being 2. Supporting Parents, Caregivers, and Youth Professionals Initiatives that equip adults with the tools and knowledge to support youth mental health, including: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Educators Youth workers Therapists Parents and guardians 3. Expanding Access to Mental Health Care Programs that improve pathways to mental health intervention, including: Early identification of mental health challenges Access to counseling or therapy School-based mental health services Community mental health programs Examples of Funded Youth Mental Health Programs The Cigna Foundation has previously supported a wide range of impactful initiatives through this grant program. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); 2024 Partner Projects Examples of impact through funded grant programs from our 2024 nonprofit partners include: A holistic, trauma-informed after-school program designed to empower teen boys and girls severely impacted by challenging life circumstances. Building social-emotional learning skills in high-school students and educating parents, caregivers, and educators about specific mental health challenges of the students. Subsidized therapy for youth, as well as mental health training for local youth-serving organizations and schools. Explore outcomes from this cohort in our Improving Youth Mental Health 2025 Impact Report . Examples of impact through funded grant programs from our 2025 nonprofit partners include: A virtual program led by medical students that helps youth in grades 6-12 build skills to manage their stress, develop an understanding of how to take care of their own mental health, and respond to peers experiencing suicidal thoughts. A Students Supporting Students program that recruits and trains peers, supported by school staff, to identify signs and symptoms of mental health issues and empowers them to lead a mental health campaign in their school. Cost-free, equitable access to school-based care, within the school setting and during school hours, using personalized treatment plans. Who Should Apply This grant opportunity is designed for nonprofit organizations that serve children and adolescents experiencing mental health challenges . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Ideal applicants include organizations that: Provide youth mental health services Deliver school-based mental health programs Support social-emotional learning Train parents, caregivers, and educators Offer early intervention or therapy services for youth Work with underserved or high-need communities Programs should be evidence-informed and focused on measurable outcomes for youth well-being. Why This Grant Matters Investing in youth mental health today helps build healthier, more resilient communities tomorrow . The Cigna Foundation believes that supporting the mental well-being of young people strengthens families, communities, and society as a whole. Through partnerships with nonprofits, the foundation aims to expand access to care, strengthen support networks, and ensure that children and teens have the resources they need to thrive . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); How to Apply Organizations interested in applying for funding can submit their applications before the deadline. The Improving Youth Mental Health Grant application is open now until March 12, 2026 at 5 pm ET. Download the Request for Application (RFA) [PDF] for details about the grant program. Register and apply now Learn More Read our frequently asked questions . Related Links The Cigna Group Foundation Related Resources Improving Youth Mental Health 2025 Impact Report 2025 Youth Mental Health Press Release 2024 Youth Mental Health Press Release ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); 1 Protecting Youth Mental Health, Department of Health and Human Services, 2021, https://www.hhs.gov/sites/default/files/surgeon-general-youth-mental-health-advisory.pdf Source https://www.thecignagroup.com/our-impact/esg/healthy-society/community/foundation/improving-youth-mental-health The post $9 Million Cigna Foundation Grant for Youth Mental Health Programs (Apply by March 12, 2026) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Africa CDC Kofi Annan Scholars Program 2026: Strategy Development Expert (AVoHC) – Apply by 2 April
summary: Public health professionals across Africa and the African diaspora can now apply for the Africa CDC Kofi Annan Scholars Program Strategy Development Expert role under the African Volunteers Health Corps (AVoHC) . This prestigious opportunity deploys experienced experts to support national public health systems, strategic planning, and health policy development across Africa . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The programme contributes to Africa s Health Security and Sovereignty agenda , strengthening the continent s ability to prevent, detect, and respond to public health threats. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Key Details Organization: Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC) Programme: Kofi Annan Scholars Program African Volunteers Health Corps (AVoHC) Role: Strategy Development Expert Number of Positions: 20 Deployment Type: On-call expert roster Contract Type: Volunteer roster with paid deployments Location: Deployments across African Union Member States Application Deadline: 2 April 2026 (11:59 PM EAT) ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Successful applicants will join a roster of Strategy Development Experts who may be deployed to support Ministries of Health, National Public Health Institutes (NPHIs), and public health institutions across Africa. About the Kofi Annan Global Health Leadership Program The Kofi Annan Global Health Leadership Program (KA-GHLP) was launched by the African Union Commission and Africa CDC in partnership with the Kofi Annan Foundation . ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); The initiative aims to build a new generation of African public health leaders capable of strengthening health systems and improving pandemic preparedness across the continent. The program includes three main components: Public Health Leadership Fellowship Public Health Scholars Program Public Health Policy Forum The Kofi Annan Scholars Program specifically deploys experienced professionals into time-bound technical assignments to strengthen national health systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Role of the Strategy Development Expert Strategy Development Experts will support governments and public health institutions in designing and implementing evidence-based health strategies and institutional frameworks. These assignments may involve developing: National public health workforce strategies Institutional development plans Health system strengthening frameworks Emergency preparedness and response strategies ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Experts will also ensure alignment with Africa CDC s New Public Health Order and national priorities. Key Responsibilities Selected experts will: Conduct situational analyses and capability assessments Identify policy and operational gaps in public health systems Draft national strategies and institutional plans Facilitate strategic planning consultations and stakeholder workshops Develop implementation roadmaps and monitoring frameworks Produce technical reports, policy briefs, and planning guidance Support resource mobilization and partner coordination Design monitoring, evaluation, and learning (MEL) systems ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Eligibility Requirements Academic Qualifications Applicants must hold a: Master s or PhD in Public Health Health Policy Health Systems Management Strategic Planning or related field Professional Experience Applicants should have: At least 7 years of experience in strategic planning or public health policy Experience working with Ministries of Health, NPHIs, or development partners Demonstrated ability to develop national strategies or institutional frameworks Knowledge of African health systems and policy environments Experience with Africa CDC initiatives or African Union frameworks such as Agenda 2063 is an advantage. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Required Skills Technical Skills Policy analysis and strategic planning Institutional development and system strengthening Stakeholder engagement and facilitation Strategic writing and policy communication Personal Competencies Adaptability in complex environments Strong cultural awareness and emotional intelligence Ability to work independently and in teams Commitment to service and impact Language Requirements Applicants must be proficient in at least one African Union working language: English French Arabic Portuguese Spanish Swahili Knowledge of multiple AU languages is an advantage. Benefits of Joining the AVoHC Roster Although membership in the African Volunteers Health Corps (AVoHC) does not provide payment during the roster period, experts receive benefits during deployments. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); During Deployment Round-trip travel tickets Health insurance Stipend and allowances Accommodation allowance (when applicable) Communications and incidentals support Certificate of recognition Additional Opportunities Professional development training Mentorship opportunities Networking with experts from 55 African Union member states Participation in high-impact public health initiatives across Africa ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Application Requirements Applicants must submit: Completed African Union CV Template Updated CV Copy of African passport Academic certificates and qualifications Only fully completed applications will be considered. Application Deadline 2 April 2026, 11:59 PM EAT Only shortlisted candidates will be contacted. Gender Equality and Inclusion Africa CDC is an equal opportunity employer , and qualified women are strongly encouraged to apply . Candidates from underrepresented African Union member states are also encouraged to apply. How to Apply Interested applicants can apply through the official African Union recruitment portal: APPLY HERE FOR MORE DETAILS PLEASE VISIT The post Africa CDC Kofi Annan Scholars Program 2026: Strategy Development Expert (AVoHC) – Apply by 2 April appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Odysseus Summer School 2026 Doctoral Seminar & Grants for PhD Students in EU Migration and Asylum Law
summary: PhD researchers working on European migration and asylum law have a unique opportunity to present their research, receive expert feedback, and potentially receive financial support through the 2026 Odysseus Summer School Doctoral Seminar. Organized by the Odysseus Academic Network for Legal Studies on Immigration and Asylum in Europe, the doctoral seminar forms part of the prestigious Odysseus Summer School program. It offers selected doctoral candidates the opportunity to engage with leading experts in migration and asylum law while strengthening their research through constructive academic dialogue. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The program is hosted by the Odysseus Network at Universit libre de Bruxelles (ULB) and aims to foster collaboration and academic excellence in European migration and asylum policy research. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About the Doctoral Seminar The doctoral seminar is designed to support PhD researchers by providing a platform to present their thesis projects to a panel of experts drawn from members of the Odysseus Network. Participants receive detailed feedback on their research topic, structure, and methodology. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); The seminar is primarily aimed at PhD students in law. However, doctoral researchers from related fields such as political science or international relations are also welcome to apply, provided their research addresses legal aspects of migration or asylum. Participants benefit from: Direct feedback from renowned academics and specialists in migration and asylum law Advice to strengthen the research design and methodology of their doctoral thesis Networking opportunities with fellow researchers and experts across Europe Exposure to current debates in EU migration and asylum law and policy ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Financial Support: Four PhD Grants Available The Odysseus Network offers four competitive grants to support PhD students attending the summer school and doctoral seminar. The grant covers: Tuition fees for the summer school (excluding the 350 deposit) Travel expenses Accommodation costs ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Please note that daily subsistence and personal expenses are not included. Grant recipients will be selected based on both their academic record and financial need. Successful applicants will also assist the coordination team with on-site organization during the summer school. This role does not interfere with their participation in the academic sessions. Eligibility Requirement Participation in the doctoral seminar is limited to individuals who have already registered for the Odysseus Summer School and paid the required deposit fee. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Applicants seeking financial support must clearly explain their need for funding as part of the application process. Application Requirements Applicants must submit a complete application package that includes: Cover page including the applicant s name, PhD title, year of research progress, and supervisor s email address ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Curriculum Vitae with detailed academic background Academic records for each year of study (if applicable) A one-page summary of the PhD research topic explaining its originality and contribution to existing knowledge A one-page overview outlining the structure of the doctoral thesis Proof of payment of the 350 deposit for the Summer School Letter of motivation (required for applicants requesting financial support) All documents must be merged into a single PDF file before submission. Application Process To apply for the doctoral seminar and grants, applicants must: Complete the registration form for the Odysseus Summer School. Carefully fill out the PhD catalogue on the Odysseus website. Submit the full application package by email to: odysseus@ulb.be Incomplete applications will not be considered. APPLY HERE. For more information about this apportunity, click here . Discover more grant opportunities, click here . Application Deadline The deadline to submit applications is 15 May 2026 . Prospective applicants are encouraged to prepare their documents early to ensure a complete and competitive submission. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Contact Information For additional details regarding the doctoral seminar or application process, applicants may contact: Odysseus Network Universit libre de Bruxelles (ULB) Email: odysseus@ulb.be The post Odysseus Summer School 2026 Doctoral Seminar Grants for PhD Students in EU Migration and Asylum Law appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: GOAL NextGen Youth Programme 2026: Global Leadership Programme for Young Changemakers (Stipend Available)
summary: GOAL s NextGen Youth Programme brings together young people from all over the world to better understand global justice issues and deepen their understanding of Global Citizenship. This programme will guide young, motivated changemakers to learn about the growing crises facing our world with a focus on climate and health systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Participants will then work together to imagine solutions for the future by creating awareness-raising materials that will feed into GOAL s public engagement activities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); When will it take place? The 2026 Programme will consist of 10 sessions that take place online, with 2 sessions in-person for Irish based participants. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Programme Dates: Sat May 23 Irish based participants in-person 11-4pm. T ue May 26 International participants online 3-5pm Thu June 4 All participants online 3-5pm Thu June 11 All participants online 3-5pm Saturday- Sunday June 20/21 Irish based participants in person retreat. Time and location TBC. Thu June 25 All participants online 3-5pm Thu July 2 All participants online 3-5pm Thu July 9 All participants online 3-5pm Thu July 16 All participants online 3-5pm In order to ensure participation of this global cohort, sessions will take place on Thursdays at 3pm Irish Time (8am Honduras, 2pm Sierra Leone, 4pm Zimbabwe, 5pm Ethiopia Uganda). ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); What will I learn? Over these 10 sessions, participants will work with peers globally to develop skills in critical thinking, intercultural communication, project management, teamwork, and the fundamentals of organising and implementing public awareness campaigns. By taking part you will be empowered to: Understand the diverse and interconnected nature of our world. Critically interrogate the systems that underpin opportunities to engage and flourish in society. Reflect upon your own role and the role of others in tackling local and global challenges. Engage in dialogue with others in a spirit of empathy, inclusivity, solidarity and collaboration. Develop the courage, confidence and resilience to speak out for change. Understand the GOAL s work and have the opportunity to connect with GOAL staff. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); What are we looking for in a candidate? To be considered, applicants must meet the following criteria: Current resident of Ireland, Ethiopia, Honduras, Iraq, Sierra Leone, Syria, Turkey, Uganda, or Zimbabwe. Comfortably fluent in English. Between 18 and 25 years old. Available for 10 seminars between May and July. Sessions will fall on Thursdays and Saturdays (see timeline for actual dates). Be open and willing to engage in teamwork in between seminars. Demonstrate basic/emerging understanding of local and global issues. Show clear initiative and desire to be a part of positive change (this could be demonstrated through volunteering, participation in local events, workshop facilitation, tutoring, creative extra-curricular activities, etc.). Interest in participating in artistic methods of learning (this could be demonstrated through a passion for drama, art, creative writing, journalism, music or any other artistic discipline). Reliable access to a device and internet is desired but not essential. If you do not have access to a device, please let us know in your application, and we will endeavour to support your participation. How do I apply? To apply, please use the fields at the bottom of the page to upload the following: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); 1. Your CV or Portfolio Keep it simple, an outline of your educational background and any voluntary and/or professional experience is sufficient. Alternatively, you may include a short portfolio with examples of your work or projects to date. 2. A 500 word cover letter addressing the following: o Tell us about yourself. Why are you applying for this programme, and why do you believe you are a strong fit? ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); o Describe the skills you bring. What strengths or experiences will you contribute, and what do you hope to gain from participating? Applications who do not follow this format will not be accepted. Or Send us a two- minute video explaining why you are applying for this programme and why you believe you would be a strong fit. Describe the skills and strenghts you can contribute, and what you hope to gain from participating. Video submissions are strongly encourage. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Please note that video submissions need to be sent separately to somalley@goal.ie with your full name and country of participation in the email subject line, alongside your CV or portfolio. The CV/portfolio is a mandatory submission alongside the video application. Applications that do not adhere to these requests will not be considered. Apply Now Closing Date Applications close on April 1st. Successful applicants will be notified of selection by May 2nd. If you are not selected to participate in this year s cohort, we encourage you to stay in touch with us for other opportunities and to apply again next year. GOAL s NextGen Youth Diversity and Inclusion GOAL is committed to widening participation and achieving equity of access to non-formal Global Citizenship Education. We welcome applications from participants with diverse identities, socio-economically diverse participants, participants with or without disabilities and participants from all ethnic backgrounds. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Stipend Eligibility In order to support participation, stipends will be available for all successful participants. Stipends are intended to cover costs of internet and data as well as any travel costs incurred during the programme. Stipends of 400 for the duration of the programme are available for all successful participants. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); Apply Now Source: https://www.goalglobal.org/nextgen-youth-recruitment-2025/ The post GOAL NextGen Youth Programme 2026: Global Leadership Programme for Young Changemakers (Stipend Available) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: UNFCCC/UNDP Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme 3.0: Global Youth Climate Leadership Programme
summary: Welcome to the Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme 3.0, a collaborative effort between UNFCCC s Youth4Capacity programme and UNDP s Youth4Climate initiative (powered by the Italian Ministry of Environment and Energy Security ) ! Young climate leaders worldwide can now apply for the Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme 3.0 , an international youth mentorship initiative designed to equip emerging changemakers with the skills, knowledge, and networks needed to drive meaningful climate action. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The programme is a collaborative initiative between the UNFCCC Youth4Capacity Programme and the UNDP Youth4Climate Initiative , powered by the Italian Ministry of Environment and Energy Security . It aims to strengthen youth leadership in climate solutions by providing hands-on training, mentorship, and global networking opportunities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); If you are passionate about climate action, sustainability, youth leadership, and environmental innovation , this programme provides an opportunity to learn directly from experts while collaborating with other youth leaders around the world. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); About the Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme The Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme 3.0 brings together a global cohort of youth who are committed to advancing climate solutions in their communities and beyond. Participants will take part in a structured learning journey that includes interactive bootcamps, peer learning opportunities, and collaborative engagement sessions . The programme is designed to help young leaders build practical skills in climate project development, leadership, advocacy, and social innovation. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); By the end of the programme, participants will be better equipped to design impactful climate initiatives, mobilize communities, and contribute to global climate action efforts. Programme Structure The programme features three intensive online bootcamps focused on essential skills for climate leadership and project development. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); 1. Project Design for Climate Action Participants will learn how to design effective climate projects, including: Developing climate solutions at local and global levels Project planning and implementation strategies Monitoring and evaluating climate initiatives Resource mobilization and fundraising approaches 2. Movement Building and Climate Advocacy This bootcamp focuses on strengthening youth-led climate movements and advocacy efforts through: Leadership and community organizing Communication strategies for climate advocacy Building inclusive and impactful climate movements Collaboration with stakeholders and partners ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); 3. Entrepreneurial Mindsets for Climate Innovation Participants will explore how entrepreneurship can drive climate solutions by learning about: Social entrepreneurship for sustainability Innovative climate business models Problem-solving and systems thinking Scaling climate solutions for greater impact Programme Format The Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme will run for one month and includes: Three online bootcamps 3 4 sessions for each bootcamp Interactive learning with climate experts and practitioners Peer-to-peer engagement through a dedicated online platform Informal networking sessions through Virtual Climate Caf s ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); This immersive learning structure ensures that participants gain both theoretical knowledge and practical tools for advancing climate action. What Participants Will Gain Participants selected for the programme will benefit from a range of learning and networking opportunities, including: Training in climate project design and implementation Skills in leadership, advocacy, and resource mobilization Exposure to global climate experts and practitioners Collaboration with a diverse international youth network Opportunities to exchange ideas through peer-to-peer learning Participation in Virtual Climate Caf networking sessions ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); These experiences are designed to help young leaders strengthen their capacity to develop, lead, and scale climate solutions in their communities. Opportunity to Attend COP31 Outstanding participants in the programme may have the exclusive opportunity to attend COP31 , the United Nations Climate Change Conference. Selected youth may also be invited to present and pitch their climate project or solution during the conference, gaining global visibility and engagement with international climate leaders and stakeholders. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Certificate of Participation Participants who successfully complete the programme will receive an official certificate of participation , recognizing their contribution and commitment to climate leadership and youth-led environmental action. Who Should Apply The programme is ideal for young climate advocates, sustainability leaders, youth organizers, students, and innovators who want to deepen their impact in climate action. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(136); }); You should consider applying if you: Are passionate about climate action and environmental sustainability Want to develop skills in project management and leadership Are interested in climate advocacy and movement building Want to collaborate with youth leaders from around the world Have ideas or initiatives focused on climate solutions How to Apply Interested applicants should complete the programme application form HERE . Selected participants will be contacted to join the programme cohort. This is a unique opportunity to learn from climate experts, collaborate with global youth leaders, and strengthen your capacity to lead climate solutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(137); }); Apply today and become part of a new generation of Climate Catalysts driving global climate action. For more global youth opportunities, fellowships, scholarships, and leadership programmes, visit: www.opportunitiesforyouth.org The post UNFCCC/UNDP Climate Catalysts Mentorship Programme 3.0: Global Youth Climate Leadership Programme appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: NASA Goddard Summer Internship for High School Students 2026: Applications Open for Students Aged 16+
summary: High school students interested in science, technology, engineering, and space exploration now have an exciting opportunity to gain hands-on experience with one of the world s leading space research institutions. Applications are currently open for the NASA Goddard Summer Internship Program 2026 , designed for high school students who are eager to explore careers in STEM. Hosted at the Goddard Space Flight Center, the program provides students with a chance to work alongside scientists, engineers, and researchers who are developing cutting-edge space and Earth science technologies. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The internship aims to inspire the next generation of innovators by exposing students to real-world projects, mentorship, and collaborative learning environments within the space science community. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Program Overview The NASA Goddard Summer Internship offers high school students the opportunity to participate in research-based and technology-focused activities during the summer. Interns will gain valuable exposure to scientific methods, engineering practices, and data-driven problem-solving. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Students selected for the program may work on projects related to space science, Earth observation, astrophysics, computer science, or engineering while learning from experienced professionals at NASA. Benefits of the Internship Participants in the program will gain several valuable experiences, including: Hands-on exposure to NASA s research and technology development Mentorship from experienced scientists, engineers, and researchers Practical experience working on real-world STEM projects Opportunities to explore potential career paths in science and engineering Networking with professionals and fellow students interested in space science ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); The internship serves as an excellent stepping stone for students who aspire to pursue future studies or careers in STEM fields. Eligibility The opportunity is open to high school students aged 16 years or older who will live within 50 miles of the NASA Goddard Space Flight Center during the summer program. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Students who are passionate about science, technology, or engineering are encouraged to apply and take advantage of this unique learning experience. Application Deadline The deadline to submit applications for the NASA Goddard Summer Internship 2026 is: April 17, 2026 Students interested in participating should ensure their applications are completed before the deadline. How to Apply Applicants can learn more about the internship and submit their application through the official link below. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Apply here For more Internship opportunities from OFY, Visit Here. The post NASA Goddard Summer Internship for High School Students 2026: Applications Open for Students Aged 16+ appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Moving Minds Alliance 2026–2027 Early Childhood Development in Crisis Research Fellowship: Funding for Researchers in Low- and Middle-Income Countries
summary: Applications are now open for the Moving Minds Alliance (MMA) 2026 2027 Early Childhood Development in Crisis (ECDiC) Research Fellowship Program , an initiative designed to support local researchers working to improve the lives of young children and caregivers affected by crisis. This fellowship aims to strengthen research and evidence on children aged 0 8 years and their caregivers living in crisis contexts , particularly in low- and middle-income countries . Through financial support and collaborative opportunities, the program empowers researchers to conduct field-relevant studies that influence policies, programs, and interventions supporting vulnerable families. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The Early Childhood Development in Crisis Research Fellowship seeks to elevate the voices and expertise of local researchers who are closely connected to the communities they study. By supporting locally driven research, the program contributes to building stronger evidence and solutions for children living in challenging environments. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Fellowship Benefits Selected fellows will receive several forms of support to help them successfully conduct their research and expand its impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); These benefits include: A one-time stipend of up to $5,000 to support research activities 12 months of tailored mentorship and guidance while carrying out field-relevant studies Opportunities to collaborate with a global network of researchers focused on early childhood development Engagement with the MMA Research Forum team to strengthen research outputs and amplify findings This combination of financial assistance, mentorship, and collaboration helps fellows produce impactful research that can influence programs supporting young children and families in crisis settings. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Focus Areas The fellowship supports research related to Early Childhood Development in Crisis contexts , particularly focusing on: Young children aged 0 8 years Caregivers supporting children in fragile or emergency settings Evidence that can inform policy, humanitarian response, and development programs ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Researchers are encouraged to design studies that address real-world challenges affecting children and families living in crisis conditions. Application Deadline The deadline to apply for the Moving Minds Alliance 2026 2027 ECDiC Research Fellowship Program is: ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); March 31, 2026 Applicants are encouraged to carefully review the program requirements before submitting their applications. How to Apply Interested researchers should review the official Terms of Reference and submit their applications through the link below. Download the Terms of Reference. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); For more Fellowship Opportunities , Visit OFY here. The post Moving Minds Alliance 2026–2027 Early Childhood Development in Crisis Research Fellowship: Funding for Researchers in Low- and Middle-Income Countries appeared first on Opportunities for Youth .

title: Apply Now: AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Fellowships & Scholarships for Youth Leaders
summary: Applications are now open for the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme (YHP) Young Leaders initiative , an opportunity designed to support young changemakers who are working to improve health, wellbeing, and opportunity for young people around the world. Through a series of Impact Fellowships and scholarships to global youth leadership events , this initiative helps emerging leaders strengthen their work, expand their networks, and amplify their impact. Open to individuals aged 18 to 35 , the Young Health Programme recognizes that young people are already driving innovative solutions to complex social and health challenges. The initiative provides a platform to support these leaders by connecting them with global partners such as One Young World and the AFS Youth Assembly , two well-known international gatherings that bring together youth leaders, policymakers, and experts from around the world. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); Participants selected through the Young Health Programme will have opportunities to attend these global events through fully supported fellowships and scholarships , where they can exchange ideas, develop leadership skills, and collaborate with other young changemakers working to create healthier communities. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); About the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme The Young Health Programme (YHP) is a global initiative led by AstraZeneca that focuses on empowering young people to take action on issues affecting youth health and wellbeing. The program works with partners around the world to support youth-led initiatives, strengthen leadership skills, and help young advocates bring their ideas to larger platforms. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Through the Young Leaders initiative , the program identifies passionate individuals who are already making a difference in their communities. Whether they are working on health education, youth empowerment, community wellbeing, environmental sustainability, or social innovation, the program seeks to support those whose work contributes to healthier and more resilient societies. Selected participants become part of a growing network of youth advocates who are collaborating to improve the future of health for young people globally. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); What the Opportunity Offers Through the Young Health Programme Young Leaders initiative, selected applicants can receive support through several pathways designed to help them grow as leaders and expand the impact of their work. Participants may receive: Scholarships to attend major global leadership events. These include gatherings such as the One Young World Summit or the AFS Youth Assembly , where youth leaders connect with experts, organizations, and policymakers from across the world. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Leadership development opportunities. Fellows and scholars gain access to training, mentoring, and learning opportunities designed to strengthen their leadership skills and strategic thinking. Global networking opportunities. Participants join an international community of changemakers working on youth health and social impact initiatives. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); Platforms to amplify their work. Through partnerships and global events, selected young leaders have opportunities to showcase their initiatives and gain visibility for the issues they care about. These experiences are designed to help young changemakers expand their reach, collaborate with others, and scale their solutions. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); Why This Opportunity Matters Young people are increasingly at the forefront of solving some of the world s most pressing challenges. From improving access to health services to promoting mental wellbeing, environmental sustainability, and social inclusion, youth-led initiatives are shaping the future of communities around the globe. However, many young leaders lack access to the resources, networks, and platforms that could help them scale their work. Opportunities like the Young Health Programme Fellowships and Scholarships help bridge that gap by connecting youth changemakers with global networks, leadership training, and spaces where their ideas can reach a wider audience. By investing in youth leadership, programs like this not only support individual changemakers but also help strengthen the global movement toward healthier, more equitable societies. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); Who Should Apply The AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Young Leaders initiative is open to young people aged 18 to 35 who are already taking action to create positive change. Ideal applicants include individuals who: Are leading or contributing to initiatives that improve youth health or wellbeing Are passionate about social impact, community development, or sustainability Want to strengthen their leadership skills and expand their networks Are eager to collaborate with other global changemakers Applicants may come from a wide variety of backgrounds, including youth organizations, nonprofits, social enterprises, health advocacy groups, or community initiatives. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); How to Apply Applications for the AstraZeneca Young Health Programme Young Leaders initiative are now open. Interested applicants should review the eligibility criteria and available fellowship and scholarship pathways on the official program page. title: Apply for the THRIVE Global Impact Challenge 2026 for AgriFood and Climate Innovation
summary: The THRIVE Global Impact Challenge 2026 is a global competition designed to identify and support startups developing innovative solutions for some of the world s most pressing challenges in agriculture, food systems, and climate sustainability. Organized by THRIVE Agrifood and SVG Ventures, the challenge seeks entrepreneurs and startups that are building technologies capable of transforming the global agri-food ecosystem. As climate change intensifies pressure on food systems, and as the global population continues to grow, innovation is becoming essential to ensure food security, sustainable farming practices, and resilient supply chains. Through the Global Impact Challenge, THRIVE connects startups with investors, corporate partners, and industry leaders who can help scale solutions that deliver measurable environmental and social impact. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(127); }); The competition forms part of the THRIVE Global Impact Initiative , a long-term effort focused on accelerating collaboration and innovation across the agriculture and food sector. The initiative brings together startups, investors, producers, and policymakers to support technologies that improve sustainability and resilience in global food systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(128); }); Who Should Apply The THRIVE Challenge is open to early-stage startups and entrepreneurs from around the world who are developing solutions that address critical challenges in agriculture and food systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(129); }); Applicants typically include startups working on innovations in areas such as: Regenerative agriculture and soil health Artificial intelligence and automation in agriculture Water and waste management solutions Clean energy and climate technologies Animal health and nutrition Post-harvest technologies and food innovation The challenge welcomes companies at the seed to Series A stage , meaning applicants should have a prototype, early product, or initial market traction with the potential to scale globally. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(130); }); Global participation is encouraged, and startups from any country can apply as long as their innovation aligns with the program s focus on sustainable agriculture and climate-positive solutions. What Selected Startups Receive Participating in the Challenge provides startups with a powerful platform to gain visibility, mentorship, and access to investors. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(131); }); Selected startups may benefit from opportunities such as: Pitching their solutions to industry leaders and investors Exposure to corporate partners and potential customers Mentorship and coaching to strengthen their pitch and strategy Participation in THRIVE s global agri-food innovation network Finalists present their innovations during the THRIVE Global Impact Summit in Silicon Valley , where leaders from across the global agrifood ecosystem gather to discuss trends, investment opportunities, and technological breakthroughs shaping the future of food systems. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(132); }); In previous editions of the challenge, winning companies have also received investment opportunities and industry recognition that can accelerate growth and global adoption of their technologies. Why This Opportunity Matters Global food systems face unprecedented pressure from climate change, resource scarcity, and rising demand for sustainable production. Addressing these challenges requires bold ideas, technological innovation, and collaboration across sectors. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(133); }); The Challenge helps bridge the gap between innovators and the global ecosystem of investors, corporations, and policymakers working to transform agriculture. By highlighting scalable solutions from regenerative farming technologies to climate-smart food production the challenge supports startups that can deliver meaningful environmental and economic impact. For entrepreneurs, the program is more than a competition. It offers access to a global platform that can help accelerate commercialization, strengthen partnerships, and bring innovative ideas to the market faster. Application Process and Timeline Startups interested in participating must submit an online application outlining their innovation, impact potential, and scalability. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(134); }); After applications close, selected companies may be invited to participate in Virtual Pitch Day , where they present their solutions to industry experts and investors. Finalists then advance to compete in the live Global Impact Challenge during the Global Impact Summit. Entrepreneurs interested in applying should carefully review the eligibility criteria and prepare clear information about their technology, market potential, and measurable impact. Applications close on 15 May 2026. To learn more about the THRIVE Global Impact Challenge and start your application, visit the official program page. ezstandalone.cmd.push(function () { ezstandalone.showAds(135); }); Looking for more opportunities? Check out the Opportunities for Youth website f or the latest global programs and initiatives.
